channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: DOGE says it found nearly untraceable budget line item responsible for $4.7T in payments
item.description: DOGE identified a nearly untraceable code linking payments to the U.S. Treasury to a budget line item, accounting for nearly $4.7 trillion in payments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 4 NYC deputy mayors resign after meeting with Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Four high-level officials in Mayor Eric Adams' administration resigned Monday after expressing concerns about the mayor's relationship with the Trump Administration, according to City Hall and sources familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Cook, PIX 11

item.title: SpaceX employees visiting FAA amid reported firings
item.description: Members of the SpaceX team will "envision how we can make a new, better, modern and safer system," Sean Duffy said.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Federal judge skeptical of effort to block Musk's DOGE from accessing data, firing employees
item.description: A federal judge expressed skepticism of arguments against President Donald Trump's DOGE and Elon Musk in a case seeking to bar access to federal data on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How President Trump is expanding the powers of the presidency
item.description: President Trump is pushing the limits of presidential power, and the courts are pushing back. MPR News host Angela Davis talks about what a president can and cannot do under the law.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Maja Beckstrom, MPR News

item.title: A fired national park ranger lost his dream job. He says the public is losing more
item.description: Brian Gibbs was an environmental educator at Effigy Mounds National Monument in Iowa. His job was among those nixed by the Trump administration as it pushes to shrink federal spending.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bowman, MPR News

item.title: Five pressing questions as RFK Jr. takes HHS reins
item.description: His entire Make America Healthy Again movement is based largely on undermining the scientific and public health messaging from the federal government, along with overhauling the status quo in the food and drug industries.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: Trump moves on budget, policy cast shadow over Minnesota Legislature
item.description: The policies and priorities of President Donald Trump can be seen in St. Paul as lawmakers consider the spilldown effects. Meanwhile, protests of Trump across the country are planned with one happening in Minnesota on President’s Day.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: US leaders in Saudi Arabia for peace talks on Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he will not accept a deal without a seat at the negotiating table.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration cuts hit FDA employees in food safety, medical devices and tobacco products
item.description: Nearly half of the FDA’s $6.9 billion budget comes from fees paid by companies the agency regulates, including drug and medical device makers, which allows the agency to hire extra scientists to swiftly review products. Eliminating those positions will not reduce government spending.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans hope to jam through defense, border funding boost ahead of shutdown fight
item.description: Senate Republicans have the ambitious goal of jamming through funding boosts for defense and border security — separately from the appropriations process — ahead of a mid-March deadline to prevent a government shutdown. Senate Budget Committee Chair Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: How a South Texas community college embraced apprenticeships to ease a growing nursing shortage
item.description: South Texas College in McAllen launched one of the first registered nursing apprenticeships in the country as area hospitals expect the need for nurses to increase.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Sneha Dey, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukraine and Europe worry about being sidelined as Trump pushes direct talks with Russia on war's end
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s approach to ending Russia’s war against Ukraine has left European allies and Ukrainian officials worried they are being largely sidelined by the new U.S. administration as Washington and Moscow plan direct negotiations.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu signals he's moving ahead with Trump's idea to transfer Palestinians from Gaza
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Sunday signaled that he was moving ahead with U.S. President Donald Trump's proposal to transfer the Palestinian population out of Gaza, calling it “the only viable plan to enable a different future" for the region.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic governors diverge on how to handle Trump
item.description: Democratic governors are diverging in their approaches to President Trump amid mounting speculation that some of them are gunning for the White House in 2028. Some are digging their heels into resistance efforts, as in Illinois, where Gov.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says 14th Amendment is for former slaves, not migrants
item.description: On Thursday, a fourth federal judge blocked the executive order issued by the president on his first day in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats' headaches multiply in battle for Senate
item.description: Correction: The dates of Sens. Warner's and Shaheen's last wins have been corrected. Minnesota Sen. Tina Smith’s (D) decision to not run for reelection in 2026 is adding another headache for Democrats trying to win back control of the Senate in two years.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Activist Curtis Sliwa joins race for NYC mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York City mayoral race just got even more crowded. The founder of the Guardian Angels nonprofit, Curtis Sliwa, has officially filed paperwork to run for the city's top job. He also ran for mayor back in 2021 and lost to the current Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: House GOP budget resolution in jeopardy amid concerns from moderates
item.description: The House GOP’s budget resolution could be in jeopardy of not clearing the full chamber amid concerns from moderates over likely cuts to social safety net programs — particularly Medicaid — with a handful undecided on whether they will support the key measure. Reps. David Valadao (R-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: George has his day, and so does Abe. But states honor US presidents in lots of ways
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — Is Presidents Day the most confusing holiday in the U.S.? States seem to have as many names for it and ideas about whom to honor as there have been presidents. The federal government doesn't even recognize Monday as Presidents Day.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida passes immigration bill, repealing in-state tuition for undocumented students
item.description: The third and final day of the special session concluded with the passage of an immigration bill, one that sparked significant debate among lawmakers in both the House and Senate.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Suspended Hillsborough Democrat defends online posts some say are antisemitic
item.description: The Hillsborough County Democratic Executive Committee has suspended a member until January 2027 over posts online and wording in email messages.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: At McMahon’s confirmation hearing, it was the Education Department on trial
item.description: In her confirmation hearing, Linda McMahon handled tough questions from Democrats, and affirmed President Trump’s plans to shrink, and potentially eliminate, the department.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner and Jonaki Mehta, MPR News

item.title: Judge blocks Trump’s gender-affirming care executive orders nationwide
item.description: BALTIMORE — A federal judge Thursday blocked parts of two executive orders issued by President Trump that collectively seek to restrict gender-affirming care. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Legislature cancels meetings, closes building as Sierra storm arrives in Nevada
item.description: While Las Vegas drivers navigate rain-soaked roads for the first time in awhile, the Nevada Legislature has shut down for the day to keep drivers out of hazardous conditions in the Reno-Carson City area.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DOJ sues NY Gov. Hochul, AG James over immigration policies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's newly installed attorney general, Pam Bondi, went after New York leaders Wednesday over the state's immigration policies, announcing a lawsuit in the latest effort by the Republican administration to carry out the president's hardline immigration campaign p
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Skyrocketing fees: St. Pete condo owners say they never asked for bailout, only relief
item.description: Condo owners are waiting for answers on whether lawmakers will do anything to ease concerns over rising fees.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bad landlords target of bill that provides tools to help Las Vegas tenants
item.description: Nevada cities and counties don't have a lot of options when a landlord ignores poor conditions that are putting tenants in danger. Lawmakers could use AB211 to change that.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk defends DOGE alongside Trump in striking Oval Office moment
item.description: Elon Musk on Tuesday defended the work of the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) alongside President Trump, who invited Musk to speak and sat at the resolute desk as the tech mogul gave a lengthy address.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Durbin calls for investigation into Patel's role in FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) is asking the Department of Justice’s (DOJ) inspector general to investigate “highly credible information” that Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to head the FBI, is already directing a “purge” of the bureau before he’s been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Pentagon changes Fort Liberty's name back to Fort Bragg
item.description: The Pentagon will change the name of the North Carolina military base Fort Liberty back to Fort Bragg, fulfilling a campaign pledge by President Trump while honoring a different eponym.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump signs executive order restoring plastic straw use in government
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order directing the federal government to return to using plastic straws.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5
